data: Learn how to make small talk with colleagues at work

message:
Hey! Today we're learning how to make small talk with colleagues at work.

Friendly small talk builds good relationships and makes the workplace more comfortable.
image_url: https://r2-activity-data.gosupernova.live/system-uploads/scenario-data/2ae64bdf-bb3c-4528-8205-c06f6d52888d-51.-Workplace-Small-Talk.jpg
button_text: Cool!

message:
When you want to start a casual conversation at work, use this structure:

"It has been [adjective] lately — how are you managing [situation]?"

For example: "It has been quite hectic lately — how are you managing the new project deadlines?"
image_url: https://r2-activity-data.gosupernova.live/system-uploads/scenario-data/2ae64bdf-bb3c-4528-8205-c06f6d52888d-51.-Workplace-Small-Talk.jpg
button_text: Got it!

message:
Here's another example.

If a colleague looks tired, you can say:

"It has been very busy lately — how are you managing the extra workload?"
image_url: https://r2-activity-data.gosupernova.live/system-uploads/scenario-data/2ae64bdf-bb3c-4528-8205-c06f6d52888d-51.-Workplace-Small-Talk.jpg
button_text: Next!

message:
This structure helps you start a warm and natural conversation.

It shows that you care about how your colleague is doing.
image_url: https://r2-activity-data.gosupernova.live/system-uploads/scenario-data/2ae64bdf-bb3c-4528-8205-c06f6d52888d-51.-Workplace-Small-Talk.jpg
button_text: Makes sense!

message:
Let's practice workplace small talk!

image_url:
button_text: Start Practice!

Select the correct way to start small talk with a colleague

1.It has been quite hectic lately — how are you managing the new project deadlines?
2.Why is the office so busy and why is everyone stressed these days?

Correct answer: 1

Select the correct way to check in with a colleague who looks tired

1.You look very tired today. Are you not sleeping well at night?
2.It has been very busy lately — how are you managing the extra workload?

Correct answer: 2
It&&has&&been quite&&hectic lately&&— how&&are&&you managing the&&new&&project&&deadlines?
It&&has&&been very&&busy lately&&— how&&are&&you managing the&&extra&&workload?
It&&has&&been quite&&challenging lately&&— how&&are&&you managing the&&new&&system&&changes?
It&&has&&been rather&&intense lately&&— how&&are&&you managing the&&back-to-back&&meetings?
It&&has&&been fairly&&stressful lately&&— how&&are&&you managing the&&tight&&schedules?

first_message: That was incredible! Now we're going to practice by answering few questions in English. Can we start?
prompt_id: 4d3f5213-fcc4-40f3-879d-051908377a31
questions: "'1. You meet a colleague in the break room. How do you start a small talk conversation?'

'2. You see a team member looking stressed before a meeting. How do you check in with them?'

'3. You are waiting with a colleague before a call starts. How do you make small talk?'

'4. A coworker just returned from leave. How do you start a friendly conversation?'

'5. You meet your manager in the hallway. How do you make a brief friendly comment?'"
